items.title: Fulham close to agreement for Man City winger Bobb
items.summary: Fulham are close to reaching an agreement to sign winger Oscar Bobb from fellow Premier League side Manchester City.

items.title: Does anyone want to win the Premier League? A 12-point gap is nothing in this crazy season
items.summary: A Man United title triumph is an incredibly unlikely scenario, but unless Arsenal, Man City or Aston Villa start performing like champions, this season could have another improbable twist.

items.title: Beating Arsenal puts Man United into Premier League title race
items.summary: Man United are 12 points behind league-leading Arsenal, but after beating the Gunners on Sunday, can the Red Devils pull off an unlikely title push?

items.title: Drie kaarten in zes seconden in Turkije: scheids laat zich niet opjagen door speler
items.summary: Kerem Demirbay van Kasimpasa ging in de afgelopen speelronde van de Turkse Süper Lig van een schone lei naar twee gele en een rode kaart in zes seconden tijd. Scheidsrechter Ali Yilmaz was het gedrag van de speler zat in de slotfase van het duel tussen Kasimpasa en Trabzonspor.

items.title: Was Chelsea's penalty at Crystal Palace a VAR error?
items.summary: Jaydee Canvot blocked Joao Pedro's goal-bound shot with his arm and a penalty was awarded after a VAR intervention - but was it the right decision?

items.title: 'Leverkusen trekt vervanger Flekken aan, Chelsea haalt verdediger terug'
items.summary: De tijd van winterse versterkingen is aangebroken, in dit blog volgen we alle transferactiviteit in het buitenland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Heading ball ‘likely’ contributed to Gordon McQueen’s brain injury, coroner finds
items.summary: Former Scotland defender, who played for Manchester United and Leeds during a 16-year career, died at his home in 2023, aged 70 Heading a football “likely” contributed to the brain injury which was a factor in the death of the former Scotland defender Gordon McQueen, a coroner has found. McQueen – who was capped for Scotland 30 times between 1974 and 1981, and played for both Manchester United and Leeds during a 16-year career – died at his home in North Yorkshire in June 2023, aged 70. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Heading ball likely contributed to McQueen's brain disease'
items.summary: An inquest concludes that "it is likely that repetitive head impacts, sustained by heading the ball while playing football, contributed to the CTE" which was a factor in the former footballer's death.

items.title: 'Let's get it on!' Wardley welcomes summer showdown against Fury
items.summary: Fabio Wardley would welcome a world heavyweight title fight against Tyson Fury this summer in a 'huge event' for British boxing, says his manager Mike Ofo.

items.title: Nederlands talent (18) dicht bij stap naar Barcelona
items.summary: Juwensley Onstein (18) is dicht bij een stap naar Barcelona. Dat bevestigen bronnen in België. De Nederlandse verdediger is nu actief voor RC Genk, maar vertrekt - mits er geen calamiteiten ontstaan - spoedig naar Spanje.

items.title: NEC laat oog vallen op Eredivisie-bekende Danilo
items.summary: NEC is in de zoektocht naar een nieuwe aanvaller uitgekomen bij Danilo Pereira. De 26-jarige spits van Rangers FC is een van de kandidaten om de voorhoede van de Nijmegenaren te versterken.

items.title: Na lange tijd weer een zorgeloze zege voor Ajax: 'Een verademing met eerder'
items.summary: Ajax heeft het zichzelf tegen FC Volendam niet al te lastig gemaakt en dat is in het verleden tijdens andere wedstrijden weleens anders geweest. Analyticus Kenneth Perez stipte dit aan en zag ook één groot verbeterpunt vergeleken met eerder dit seizoen.

items.title: WSL talking points: Liverpool finally get first win but are Chelsea out of title race?
items.summary: With Chelsea stumbling at the hands of Arsenal at the top, bottom club Liverpool finally ended their long wait The Arsenal head coach, Renée Slegers, was effusive in her praise of her players’ ability to make things happen when their backs are firmly against the wall. After a dominant 2-0 win over Chelsea at Stamford Bridge she was asked about the Gunners’ ability to defy the odds and win against a major rival despite several key squad members absent through injury and suspension. “When the moment is there for this team, when it really, really, really has to happen, they do it every single time, and so there’s a lot of strength in this team in those moments,” she said. That ability is great, but also a little damning. Five draws this season have already done the damage of practically ruling Arsenal out of the title race, one point separating them from Saturday’s opponents but 10 between them and the league leaders, Manchester City. It is not enough to find the fire, clarity and focus you need when up against the wall, Arsenal need to find it far sooner if they are to properly challenge for a title they’ve not won in seven years. Suzanne Wrack Match report: Chelsea 0-2 Arsenal Match report: London City Lionesses 1-2 Manchester City Continue reading...

items.title: Leeds Rhinos duo Sykes and Northrop heading to Australia
items.summary: Leeds Rhinos Women have announced Bella Sykes and Izzy Northrop are both leaving the club to take up new opportunities in Australia.

items.title: Liverpool pull out of talks with Spurs over Andy Robertson transfer
items.summary: Liverpool have no cover for Robertson and Milos Kerkez Left-back, whose deal ends in June, has not asked to go Liverpool have pulled out of talks with Tottenham over their approach for Andy Robertson. Spurs made their move late last week as Thomas Frank sought to add much-needed experience to his squad. Liverpool listened to the approach in recognition of Robertson’s contribution to the club in the last eight and a half years and the fact he is out of contract in the summer. Continue reading...

items.title: Scotland to face Ivory Coast in latest World Cup warm-up
items.summary: Scotland will play the Ivory Coast at Everton's Hill Dickinson Stadium on March 31.

items.title: Rooney: Arteta 'panicked' in Man United defeat
items.summary: Wayne Rooney believes that Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta "panicked" during his side's 3-2 defeat at home to Manchester United on Sunday.

items.title: Arteta challenges 'poor' Arsenal after Man Utd loss
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has challenged Arsenal to show the mental strength to win the Premier League after falling to a nervous 3-2 home defeat against Manchester United.

items.title: Oviedo coach: Yamal goal from 'another galaxy'
items.summary: Real Oviedo coach Guillermo Almada branded Lamine Yamal a player from "another galaxy" after the Barcelona forward's wonder goal in Sunday's LaLiga meeting between the two sides.
